_README: Human curation layer for the answer pool, keyed by Wikidata QID. build-data.mjs deep-merges these fields on top of each generated record right before writing puzzles.json, so manual fixes survive `npm run build:data`. Supported per-QID keys: any Puzzle field (hints, aliases, answer, birth, death) to override the generated value, plus the control keys `exclude` (true = drop from the pool, supersedes the old in-code BLOCKLIST) and `note` (free-text rationale, ignored by the build). An override that supplies fields implies the entry is human-reviewed, so its needsReview flag is forced false. The build warns about stale overrides whose QID never appears in the pool. Keys starting with underscore are ignored.

hints: German writer, artist, natural scientist and politician.
Q5879.note: Wikidata lists 'politician' first; Goethe is famous as a writer.

hints: Austrian psychiatrist and founder of psychoanalysis.
Q9215.note: Declared occupation order picks 'essayist'; he's a psychiatrist.

Q5685.note: Picked 'physician'; Chekhov is famous as a dramatist/author.

hints: Father of formal logic, he wrote foundational works on physics, biology, ethics, and poetics.
Q868.note: Polymath picked 'biologist'; primarily a philosopher. Hints hand-curated vague->specific.

Q9061.note: 'figure in science' is a stretch; he's a philosopher/economist.

Q7245.note: Picked 'journalist'; better known as an author.

Q9682.note: Generated position label was the obscure 'Monarch of the Dominion of Ceylon'.

Q307.note: Reviewed: 'science/Astronomer' is correct; the polymath auto-flag is a harmless false positive.

Q762.note: Reviewed: 'artist/Painter' is correct; the polymath auto-flag is a harmless false positive.

hints: Painted the breathtaking ceiling of the Sistine Chapel in the Vatican.
Spanish painter and sculptor best known for co-founding Cubism.
